latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
6 Feb 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9378R
Hammerson PLC
06 February 2026
6 February 2026
Hammerson plc
Directorate Change
Hammerson plc ("Hammerson") is pleased to announce the appointment of Michelle McGrath as an Independent Non-Executive Director with effect from 9 March 2026.
Michelle's appointment further strengthens the Board's experience across property and capital markets. Most recently, Michelle was Executive Director at Shaftesbury Capital plc, responsible for the group's property portfolio, including investment, asset management, leasing and marketing. Prior to its merger with Shaftesbury PLC in March 2023, Michelle was a Director of Capital & Counties Properties PLC, responsible for its property portfolio.
Michelle is an experienced corporate financier, with a background across corporate broking, equity capital markets, and mergers and acquisitions, having advised companies within the UK listed real estate sector at UBS Investment Bank.
Upon appointment, Michelle will become a member of the Remuneration and Nomination & Governance Committees.
The Board also announces that Robert Noel will step down as a member of the Remuneration Committee with effect from 9 March 2026.
Notes
Michelle was a Director of Capital & Counties Properties PLC from February 2020 to March 2023. There is no other information to be disclosed for the purposes of Listing Rule 6.4.8.13R in relation to Michelle's appointment as a Director of Hammerson.
ENDS
For further information contact:
Josh Warren
Director of Group Performance and Investor Relations
E:
josh.warren@hammerson.com
This announcement has also been released on the SENS system of the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and on Euronext Dublin.

latest.content:
28 Jan 2026 09:00
RNS Number : 6074Q
Hammerson PLC
28 January 2026
Hammerson plc ("Hammerson" or the "Company")
Notification of Full Year Results
28 January 2026
2025 Full Year Results
Hammerson will announce its full year results for the year ended 31 December 2025 on Wednesday, 25 February 2026.
The Company's full year results presentation to analysts will be held at 8.00 am at the offices of Peel Hunt, 100 Liverpool St, London EC2M 2AT. Full details of the Company's full year results presentation, including joining instructions to the analysts briefing, will be available on our website (
https://www.hammerson.com/investors/financial-calendar
) in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Hammerson plc runs a portfolio of UK shopping centres, owning and managing retail spaces that house high‑profile anchor tenants such as supermarkets, department stores and entertainment venues.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Its sites sit in key city centres and commuter hubs, giving it a strong footfall base that remains attractive to both consumers and brands. The REIT structure allows it to offer regular dividend income while leveraging its property assets for long‑term capital growth.
company_30s.current_state: After a steep decline, the company is under new leadership and has begun a buy‑back programme aimed at boosting share value and signalling confidence. The dividend outlook is solid, and the firm is actively looking to optimise its portfolio through acquisitions and asset sales.

rns_narrative: Leadership overhaul – a new CEO has taken the helm, signalling a fresh strategic direction.
Financial turnaround – the latest results show stronger earnings and a more attractive dividend payout.
Strategic expansion – the acquisition of The Oracle adds a high‑quality asset to the portfolio, while share‑holding changes reflect a shift in investor composition.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Buyback optimism amid a new CEO’s first moves
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Investors are praising the buy‑back initiative and the dividend outlook, believing the new management will unlock value from the property portfolio.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some traders worry that retail footfall is still weak, that debt levels could pressure the balance sheet, and that a takeover is still a possibility that could destabilise the current strategy.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has swung from neutral to cautiously bullish after the results announcement, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – the public interest in the stock is essentially zero, creating a perfect stage for surprise moves.

near_term_catalysts: The formal dividend declaration – a clear cash reward that can lift sentiment.
Completion of the The Oracle acquisition – adding a premium asset that could drive a price bump.
A surge in trading volume that breaks the current support level, indicating capitulation has ended.
Any announcement that the buy‑back programme will be accelerated or that new capital will be deployed.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained 3‑day volume spike, a price breakout above the recent low, and any corporate communication that confirms the buy‑back schedule.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of a mean‑reversion cycle. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, so a breakout could be imminent once the market digests the new CEO’s plans and the dividend news.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company’s deep plunge has left institutional investors on the sidelines, while the new CEO’s buy‑back and dividend strategy signals confidence. Historically, deep crash signals have produced the largest rebounds, and the current setup matches that profile. If the share climbs back to roughly the midpoint of its historic high, the upside is well over 70% from today’s level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Retail footfall remains a risk; a continued decline in shoppers could hurt lease income. Elevated debt could limit the ability to sustain buy‑backs, and any takeover speculation could trigger a sudden sell‑off.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped near the current level, while upside could reach 4–5 times that amount if the price recovers halfway to the peak. The asymmetry favours a cautious long position.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the recent low with a stop a few pips below that low. Allocate 10–15% of the account to keep risk manageable. Use a trailing stop once the price moves 20% above entry to lock in gains.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the dividend is announced or until volume shows a clear reversal. A price move above the 20‑day moving average coupled with a 5‑day volume spike would be a strong trigger.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risk has surfaced.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a steep fall, a new CEO steering a buy‑back and dividend push, the stock offers a highly asymmetric upside if it regains half its historic peak.
